steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the overall structure of the passage. Map what each part does: the first half describes Byrne and colleagues' study using historical DNA; the second half (after 'Although') explains why this approach is rarely used.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'presents a study and its approach, then describes a barrier to widespread adoption': Matches exactly — the study is described, then hDNA degradation is named as the barrier preventing broader use. ✓
  • Choice B — 'evaluates the methodology, then explains how scientists may overcome difficulties': The passage says the degradation problem is "not easily remediable" — it offers no path forward. ✗
  • Choice C — 'summarizes findings, then discusses replication': No findings are reported, and replication is never mentioned. ✗
  • Choice D — 'exemplifies a common method, then details difficulties in adapting it': The passage calls hDNA a "relatively underutilized resource," so it is not common; and the difficulty is degradation, not adaptation. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
